description: A specialized museum showcasing the history and culture of the Six Dynasties, featuring modern architectural design.
A commercial street district with a Republic of China-era charm, offering dining, entertainment, and shopping; its vibrant night views make it a popular gathering spot for young people.
A double-deck railway and highway bridge independently designed and built by China, serving as a city landmark.
The mother river of Nanjing, where night cruises on painted boats offer views of ancient architectural complexes along the banks.
A famous snack street in Nanjing, gathering authentic local delicacies and recommended by locals as a food destination.
Showcasing the intangible cultural heritage of Nanjing Yunjin brocade, with demonstrations of Yunjin weaving techniques.

attraction_name: Sun Yat-sen Mausoleum Music Stage in Zhongshan Scenic Area
description: An open-air music stage built during the Republic of China era, featuring exquisite architecture and a serene surrounding environment.
A double-spiral pedestrian bridge over the Yangtze River, a modern architectural landmark offering views of the river and the city skyline.
A sacred site commemorating the Chinese New Democratic Revolution, a solemn place to honor revolutionary martyrs.
A famous ancient temple in Nanjing, now a national revolutionary martyrs' cemetery, known for its tranquil environment and the iconic Linggu Pagoda.
China's first national institution of higher learning, located in the core scenic area of the Qinhuai River, with well-preserved ancient architecture.
A cluster of Republic of China-era mansion buildings, a well-preserved historic district with elegant architectural styles.
Nanjing's premier shopping center, featuring internationally renowned brands, located at the heart of the Xinjiekou commercial district.
The ruins of a Southern Dynasty ancient temple, now a cemetery for fallen soldiers of the National Revolutionary Army, with the Linggu Pagoda as its iconic structure.
China's largest imperial garden lake, known as the 'Pearl of Jinling,' offering diverse scenery throughout the seasons.
The official residence of Chiang Kai-shek and Soong Mei-ling, featuring French architectural style and known as the 'First Villa of the Far East.'
